title: Epidendrum stroebelii
taxonomy: Epidendrum stroebelii belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. This orchid species is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, and its exact classification is as follows: Epidendrum stroebelii (Pfitzer) H.G. Jones, 1960. The genus Epidendrum includes over 1,500 species of orchids, which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. Within the genus Epidendrum, the species stroebelii is a type of terrestrial orchid.
uses: Epidendrum stroebelii has been reported to have potential medicinal uses, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. Additionally, the plant has been researched for its antioxidant properties, which could have applications in treating various health conditions. However, more in-depth studies are needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Epidendrum stroebelii. Further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.

side_effects: Epidendrum stroebelii is not a known medicinal plant, and therefore, it does not have any reported side effects.
